q: Baroreceptors in the aortic arch detect a rise in blood pressure. What response do they trigger?
options: Increased sympathetic output to the heart, raising heart rate further
Activation of the adrenal cortex to release aldosterone and retain sodium
Increased parasympathetic (vagus nerve) signals to the heart, reducing heart rate to lower blood pressure
Chemoreceptors in the medulla stimulate vasoconstriction to redistribute blood
explanation: When baroreceptors sense elevated blood pressure, they signal the cardiovascular center in the medulla to increase vagal (parasympathetic) tone, slowing the heart and reducing blood pressure.
